errorMsgs.addSatellitesOrSelect: Add Satellites to List or Select a Satellite

help.overview: The Satellite Timeline is the mirror image of the Sensor Timeline: it shows when one selected sensor can see a list of satellites. Each row is a satellite from your watchlist (or the currently selected satellite) and each colored bar is a pass, so you can schedule a single site's observations across many targets.
help.imgAlt: The Satellite Timeline showing color coded passes for several satellites
help.imgCaption: Each row is a satellite; bar color shows the quality of each pass over the selected sensor.
help.reading:
• Green: a long pass, above the average pass threshold.
  • Yellow: an average pass.
  • Red: a short pass, below the bad pass threshold.
  • Light gray row: that satellite never enters the sensor's field of view in the window.
Hover a bar for the exact start and end times. Rows are labeled with each satellite's NORAD catalog number.
help.settings: The settings panel controls the calculation: Calculation Length (Hours) sets the window, Calculation Interval (Seconds) trades speed for precision, and the Bad Pass Length and Average Pass Length thresholds define the red, yellow, and green coloring.
help.howToUse:
1. Select a sensor; the bottom icon stays disabled until one is selected.
  2. Add satellites to the Watchlist, or select a single satellite, then open the Satellite Timeline from the bottom menu.
  3. The timeline computes and draws automatically; adjust the settings panel to change the window or thresholds.
  4. Click a pass to select that satellite and jump the clock to the pass start.
  5. Use the download button to save the timeline as a PNG image.
help.tip1: Populate the Watchlist first: with several satellites listed you can plan a whole observing session for the site in one view.
help.tip2: The timeline defaults to a 6 hour window, which suits a single night shift; raise the calculation length for longer planning horizons.
